complaint:
I am writing to formally bring to your attention a disturbing incident of animal cruelty that occurred inside the campus of Saveetha University, Chennai, Tamil Nadu.

On the university premises, specifically in the canteen area, a group of security guards reportedly captured and brutally killed a stray puppy. The act was carried out in a violent and inhumane manner within the campus grounds. This incident has caused significant distress among students and individuals who witnessed or later came to know about the event.

The puppy was a harmless stray animal that had been living around the campus. Instead of seeking assistance from authorized animal welfare organizations or following humane methods for handling stray animals, the individuals involved resorted to extreme cruelty, resulting in the death of the puppy. Such actions are deeply concerning and constitute a serious violation of animal protection laws in India.

This act appears to be a clear violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, which prohibits causing unnecessary pain or suffering to animals. Incidents like this not only demonstrate disregard for animal life but also create an unsafe and insensitive environment within an educational institution.

I respectfully request the Animal Welfare Board of India to kindly investigate this matter and take appropriate action against the individuals responsible for this cruel act. It would also be helpful if the university administration is instructed to implement proper guidelines for the humane handling and protection of stray animals on campus.
place_of_incident:
Saveetha Engineering College
Saveetha Nagar, Thandalam,
Chennai - Bangalore National Highway,
Kanchipuram

complaint:
The apartment association of Hiranandani Township in Chennai along with the local police has recently taken illegal and unethical steps against community animal feeders. In a meeting held on October 7 2025 led by Inspector Venkatesan the residents and association members were wrongly informed that feeding community dogs within the township is prohibited and any feeders will face police complaints. Following this, posters have been put up all over the township warning that feeding community animals within the premises will attract police action. This is clear intimidation of caregivers and a gross misrepresentation of the Supreme Court order.

The Supreme Court has never imposed a blanket ban on feeding community dogs. It has only directed that feeding must be done at designated spots to avoid inconvenience to residents. These designated spots are meant to be within the premises in areas with limited human movement. However the apartment association and the inspector have twisted this ruling to suit their own agenda by completely banning feeding within the township and directing feeders to go three kilometers away to feed the animals. This is not only illegal but also impractical and cruel. The community dogs are part of this township ecosystem and shifting them far away is against both AWBI and Supreme Court guidelines.

Such actions violate the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and the standing directions of the Animal Welfare Board of India which mandate that feeding spots for community animals be identified within every residential complex. The association and the police have deliberately spread false information and threatened legal action against feeders creating fear and hostility in the community.

I request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take strict action against the Hiranandani Apartment Association and Inspector Venkatesan for violating AWBI and Supreme Court guidelines intimidating animal caregivers and propagating false interpretations of the law. Immediate intervention is needed to remove the illegal posters stop the harassment of feeders and ensure that designated feeding areas are established within the township in compliance with AWBI regulations.
place_of_incident: Post, IT Park, 5/63 Old Mahabalipuram Road, Egattur Village, Opposite Siruseri, Thazhambur, Chennai, 600130, India

2 dogs caught by zone 7 dog catching team was taken to kanamapet and were kept for 15 days and were released on 5 01 2025 Of the two dogs only one was operated and the other dog was not operated  The operated dog was released with open wound The dogs ought to be released only after the would has healed completely Moreover why was the dog unfit for the surgery kept in the pound for 15 days On 7 01 2025 I spoke with Dr Srividhya as she has blocked me on whatsapp Issue 3 Mr Ashok is a community stray dog feeder in zone 10 He along with his wife dropped two male stray dogs in kanamapet pound which is allocated for Animal birth control procedure of zone 10 dogs on October 23 2024 for ABC surgery  On October 30 He received a call from Kanamapet pound for take the dog back and he collected them the same day He noticed that out of two dogs only one dogs ear was notched and a white dogs ear was not notched Upon inquiry about the absence of notch on the white dog Dr Srividhya the kanamapet pound incharge has mentioned that the dog was operated indeed and the notch is not mandatory from now on as GCC has started to chip the dogs with all details. Believing Dr Srividhya Mr Ashok released the dog the same day on October 30 2024  But on Nov 2  just 2 days after release the same white dog was picked up by GCC again and was taken to kanamapet pound for the second time he was released back on November 8 with notch in his right ear The dog was sick soon after he was released, he refused to consume food and died a couple of days later We do not know if the dog was cut open operated twice by the Greater Chennai Corporation or if the dog was not operated during the first sojourn in the kanamapet pound If the dog was not operated then Dr Srividhya has lied that the dog has been operated However we understand that the GCC is still performing the ear notch on operated dogs as pet the ABC 2023 rules Ear notch is the Visual identification of the already spayed neutered dogs But we are unaware of the reason behind the statement by Dr Srividhya about the Chipping of the dogs instead of the ear notch The capturing of dog and locking up of the dog twice in the pound was completely unnecessary The Death of this dog is the direct result of wilful negligence of the kanamapet pound incharge Dr Srividhya

complaint:
This is to bring to your attention the unlawful and unethical actions of the Tiara Hiranandani apartment association its committee members and certain residents. These individuals have been perpetuating a false scare of rabies and dog attacks within the community using this fabricated narrative to justify their cruelty toward community animals. Hate messages targeting the animals have been widely circulated on public forums by these individuals. They have gone to the extent of organizing an illegal petition to gather support for relocating/culling/disappearing of the animals which can be found at the following link https://chng.it/y9Gpq7mbrm

This petition is based on fake claims and a fabricated narrative openly calling for the unconstitutional and illegal relocation of over 100 community animals. Such actions directly violate multiple Supreme Court orders the Animal Birth Control ABC Rules 2023 and various High Court rulings that uphold the animals right to reside in their natural territory. Over the years several community dogs in Tiara Hiranandani have been harmed without any provocation or reason of their own. These cruel acts have created an atmosphere of fear and hostility against the animals further escalating the human-animal conflict in the area. While there may be one-off incidents of human-animal conflict these isolated cases do not in any way justify the illegal relocating/culling/disappearing as indirectly demanded in the petition in all of the apartment public forums. The residents involved in these activities have a history of harassing and attacking community dogs. Their current actions including the petition and the spread of misinformation are deliberate attempts to exploit fear and manipulate the situation to justify illegal relocation and cruelty. Such actions are not only unlawful but also inhumane and contrary to the principles of coexistence and animal welfare. The Supreme Court of India has clearly stated that animals have the right to reside in their territory and any relocation or harm inflicted on them is a punishable offense. I request the Animal Welfare Board of India AWBI to Take immediate and strict action against the Tiara Hiranandani apartment association its committee members and the individuals responsible for organizing the illegal petition Direct the concerned authorities to take down the illegal petition being circulated on public forums Investigate and address the harm caused to community dogs and ensure their safety and protection under the law Ensure the enforcement of the Supreme Court orders ABC Rules and other relevant laws to protect the animals rights Furthermore I request the Registrar of Societies to Investigate the apartment association for promoting and engaging in illegal activities Dissolve the association for violating the law and fostering a hostile environment that incites cruelty against animals Community animals are an integral part of our ecosystem and have as much right to live in their natural territory as any human. The actions of the association and its members including the unconstitutional demand for the illegal relocation of 100 animals must be stopped immediately to ensure justice and harmony in the community. Your prompt intervention in this matter is crucial to safeguarding the welfare of the animals and upholding the rule of law
place_of_incident: Post, IT Park, 5/63 Old Mahabalipuram Road, Egattur Village, Opposite Siruseri, Thazhambur, Chennai, 600130, India

complaint:
Sharai Benny 
No. 11/4, 1st Street  Tharageshwari Nagar  Sembakkam, 600073  
Date : 26-10-2024
To  
The Animal Welfare Corporation Trust  
Subject: Appeal for Assistance and Justice in Protecting Street Animals

Dear sir/Madam,  
I am Sharai Benny, residing at the address mentioned above. My family and I have been providing shelter and food for street animals in our community, motivated by a sense of social responsibility and kindness towards these vulnerable creatures. Currently, There are 10 dogs and 15 cats, most of whom are strays, while only 4 dogs belong to us even they are Mongrel that belong to this community. 

We have opened our home to these animals to protect them from harm, especially from a neighbor who has been aggressive and abusive towards them, even resorting to physical violence. This neighbor poses a significant threat to the safety of the animals, and there have been incidents where he has attacked them with stones and threatened those who try to intervene.
Recently, we received a notice from the corporation because this neighbor has tactically installed a CCTV camera and submitted footage of our dog on the street, claiming we are responsible for the strays. This is the same individual who, prior to installing the camera, threatened and assaulted the dogs in our compound. We have video evidence of his actions and are prepared to submit it if required.
I kindly request that the corporation handle this matter justly. Our efforts to care for these animals have been driven purely by a desire to fulfill our social responsibility, and we were unaware of any legal requirements or licensing needed for such actions. We have personally funded vaccinations and necessary birth control measures to manage the stray population, aiming to ensure the safety and well-being of these animals within our home, rather than on the streets where they face harm.

It is disheartening that some neighbors do not share this compassionate perspective and instead engage in harmful actions against animals. I urge the corporation to support us in navigating this situation and to provide guidance on how we can continue to care for these animals, including any required licenses or legal considerations.
There is no compound wall in the house, hence all the stray dogs enter the house and sleep in our plot fearing the neighbor and the corporation is asking us to take the responsibility as we have let them in. Just because we question the neighbor when he hits the dog, he has done this. We live in this house for the past 12 years and now the neighbor is threatening the house owner as well with the support of local authorities because of which our owner has asked us to vacate the house in three months. Now, if we leave this place, I am sure This guy would kill the dogs as there will be no one to question him. I would have happily let all the dogs on the street if it was a safe place for them just because of this Man I use to not chase the pets outside the house. People lack education and I am tired of living with them as I am being harassed continuously. Now I am here having no place to take all these pets because I am scared, he would kill them. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your kind assistance and support.
Contact : 6381701665

Sincerely,  
Sharai Benny

complaint:
There has been an increase in the street dogs population in my area in the recent times. These dogs have multiplied rapidly due delivering 8 to 12 puppies in each birth. These dogs wandering the streets can carry zoonotic diseases like rabies and toxoplasmosis. Moreover, they can cause property damage or environmental contamination. When feeling threatened, they may display aggression, leading to potential harm to humans and other animals. There are 40 plus families residing in our area with 80 plus kids in total. In addition to this, there is a Kindergarten named Chisel Montessori School run in the same street with more than 60 kids walking around the street every day. 

Barking, howling, and dog fights disturb these children and there were 2-3 dog bites reported in the area. They can pose a threat to public health by spreading rabies and other diseases, they can cause damage to livestock and wildlife, or they may behave aggressively towards people.

I would request your help in sterilizing the female dogs via Animal Birth Control ABC programme and also vaccinate all the street dogs in our area so that the residents are free from panic.

Manual register is maintained with improper and manipulated information to escape from the complaint. Particularly, vet Janaki does not visit the ABC centre on daily basis. That is why records are not maintained purposely to leave a loop hole. Few dogs are released beyond the boundary of Tambaram corporation and in the outskirts of the city.

complaint:
This is to bring it to your attention about the violation of AWBI rules being continuously done by Tambaram corporation undeterred for quite a while. As a result many community dogs are dying in tambaram corporation pounds.  I myself wrote to AWBI about how killing of dogs in the name of ABC happening in Tambaram corporation 8 months ago on 10/07/2023. But no actions were taken neither by AWBI nor by state animal welfare board. No one was held accountable for the dog that fought for the life with intestines out on the floor in bits and pieces without medical help for 4 days in Tambaram pound. Instead the Vet who was alleged by the Tambaram corporation to have done the surgery for the specific dog was given a permanent employment in Greater chennai corporation recently. 
Now International trust of peace is the NGO that has taken up the ABC contract in Tambaram corporation

Tambaram corporation caught a previously spayed dog with visible ear notch on her right ears. Dr Janaki split open the dog and performed a surgery looking for uterus on already spayed dog. The dog was kept on post op care for 10 days and disappeared mysteriously. This dog had a feeder and care taker who took pains to spay her in a private clinic yet the Tambaram corporation abused the dog in the name of ABC. The following question arises after this dog lost her life due to human incapacity. Why was already spayed dog caught by Tambaram corporation? Why did Dr Janaki reoperated the dog ? Is she not aware of the meaning of notches on the right ear, Why is she still employed by International trust of peace even though she has done a grave mistake. This is not a silly mistake that one could shrug off and move on. Why has Tambaram corporation not revoked the license of International trust of peace. 
Between 29October, 2023 - 05Nov, 2023 five dogs died in the pound soon after the ABC surgery done by Dr Janaki. 5 dogs died in a span of 7 days in the said period and this was brought to the attention of the corporation officials. This is not just one time occurrence and is only an example of how the ABC happens in Tambaram corporation.
A fully pregnant dog was caught by Tambaram corporation on Nov2023 on orders from Dr Janaki. The dog gave birth to 7 puppies and was locked up in the pound for 2 months by Dr Janaki and International trust of peace without any action. Only three puppies survived the ordeal. After 2 months one of the animal welfare volunteer rescued the mother and the 3 puppies from the clutches of Tambaram corporation.

The veterinary doctor Dr Janaki is a full time employee of Greater Chennai corporation and is a zonal vet of zone 5. Dr Janaki is also employed by International trust of peace which has the license to perform Animal birth control surgeries in tambaram corporation. How is it possible for  Dr Janaki to perform her full day job in greater chennai corporation and also perform surgeries during the very same day time in Tambaram corporation. Since Dr Janaki has a full time  employment in GCC which she has taken for granted and does surgeries in Tambaram corporation, she does surgeries only.  she does not do any post operative care in the tambaram pound. This is a clear case of negligence of duty in both the corporations. 

Tambaram corporation/ Pranimithran NGO also has caught fully pregnant dogs and has locked them up in the cages in Tambaram pounds twice. During the two occasions the dogs gave birth immediately after this catching, dragging and locking up ordeal. AWBI clearly states that pregnant dogs are not to be caught yet Tambaram corporation keeps repeating this inhumane catching. Tambaram has recently signed a contract with NGO named pranimithran for ABC. Pranimithran NGO is the infamous NGO that were responsible for deaths of many dogs anfd suspended in Coimbatore corporation and Avadi corporation. 

The killing spree continues unchallenged in Tambaram corporation inspite of many complaints raised by us. I request AWBI to intervene and take this issue at highest priority to put an end to this farce. I along with many animal welfare activist are knocking your door for the second time with full hope that all those dogs that died miserably in the hands of ill educated greedy veterinarians will get justice. Furthermore no other dog should end its life in the most inhumane way in Tambaram corporation.
place_of_incident: Tambaram corporation ABC center

complaint:
Subject : Cruelty to stray dogs, illegal catching and illegal relocation of homeless dogs at Paravai Town Panchayat, madurai district, Tamil Nadu regarding, 

Greetings sir/madam, 

I received a complaint on 25.07.2023 evening about illegally homeless dogs were captured by unauthorised persons at Paravai, madurai district. They were used a Ashok Leyland Ltd dost vehicle no TN 59 CF 5841 full covered by plastic rexine. 

Immediately i gave complaint letter to Samayanallur police station inspector Ms. Ratha Mahesh madam. 

Inspector informed me that this illegal relocation of homeless dogs at Paravai has been done by Paravai Town Panchayat. I received CSR copy only. Subsequently I went Paravai ABC centre along with sanitary officer Mr Ram with the knowledge of paravai Town panchayat. I inspected that the ABC centre looked like a storage room, no proper infrastructure which are given in ABC Rule 2001. Here I have attached the Paravai ABC centre video. On this worst condition of ABC centre how had planned paravai Town panchayat to caught the stray dogs for two days 24, 25.7.2023. I asked sanitary officer about that. He told that they caught stray dogs and had relocated into hills side. 

In Rule 7, ABC Rule 2001 stray dogs populations can only be managed via human method capture - sterilization - vaccination - release program  ABC and ARV programme, Supreme court only held that this is the only method to be used. 
This is the violation of the ABC Rules 2001, PCA Act 1960. 

But still now I didnt receive FIR copy for my complaint. It takes almost 53 days. 

I Hope you to take necessary steps on Paravai Town Panchayat officials. I have sent my complaint mail to madurai district collector, Superintendent of police, sp Nodal officer, AWBI. 

Here I have attached the videos and vehicle details and complaint letter, CSR copy. 

Thanking you madam. 

Yours truly, 
Murugeswari s, 
HAWR - AWBI, 
SKGSG vice president, 
Madurai 625011, 
Tamilnadu. 
9944942320.

complaint:
Subject : Illegal catching and killing of community dogs at kadayanallur municipality, Tenkasi district regarding, 

Respected sir, Vanakkam, 

 I saw the paper news on 26.08.2023 about catching of 82 homeless dogs at kadayanallur Taluka, Tenkasi district. In this today 26.8.2023 daily malar Tenkasi news Kadayanallur commissioner suganthi told that catching of stray dogs will be continued. Kadayanallur municipality has given news about catching of homeless dogs in 25.08.2023 newspaper. They has arranged 20 persons from kovil patti to catch these homeless dogs. Against the PCA Act, ABC Rule, homeless dogs are illegally captured by Kadayanallur municipality. This is offence. This is well planned execution of removing homeless dogs from Kadayanallur municipality. 
In Kadayanallur, the SDPI party blockaded the municipality to control the rabid dogs on 26.08.2023. Its led to catch upto 82 stray dogs at kadayanallur. 
I received the information regarding that on behalf of the Humanist Peoples Party, a petition was submitted to the District Collector under the leadership of District President Mohammad Yakub, requesting compensation for the boy belonging to a poor family who was bitten by a dog in Kadayanallur yesterday.
Even though these incidents were brought into your knowledge, Kadayanallur Taluka officials did against the animal welfare laws. 

On Jan 6,2023 District AntiRabies Vaccination Awareness Camp was held at Kadayanallur Government Boys High School on behalf of Tenkasi District Veterinary Department. As advised by Tirunelveli Zonal Joint Director Ponvel, Thenkasi Division Veterinary Assistant Director Uma Maheshwari, Kadayanallur Municipal Chairman Moopan Habib Rahman, Municipal Commissioner Ravichandran, Health Officer Ilango, Health Inspector Sakthivel, Veterinarian Q Imtiaz, Doctor Sivakumar, Kadayanallur Government Veterinarian M. Asan Kasim. , Veterinary Inspector Hajira, Medical Assistant Arumugam, Kadayanallur Village Assistant Senthamil Selvan, Government Boys High School Headmaster Mangaladurai, Shanmugasundarampalli Management Committee Member Tenkasi District Muslim Womens Aid Society Patent Member, Social Activist Thyagarajan, school teachers and students were present.

Here I have attached the paper news 25.08.2023,26.08.2024. videos those are collected from fb page, link and photos
 news about illegal cruel catching of homeless dogs at kadayanallur municipality, Tenkasi district. SDPI party give news to starts their protest condemning against stray dogs at Palani media whatsapp group. Is rules and regulations for stray dogs only for paper not for save Voiceless. 

I sent complaint mail regarding this illegal catching at kadayanallur municipality to Tenkasi district Collector on 26.08.2023, Kadayanallur municipality commissioner, SP NRI cell chennai, Animal Welfare board. 

On 26.08.2023 I contacted Tenkasi district joint director animal husbandry department and kadayanallur municipality commissioner. RJD told me that dont know about it. 

Still now no reply mail for my complaint from SP NRI cell chennai. 

I request you to take necessary steps to take immediate actions kadayanallur municipality commissioner and A.Yasserkahan SDPI party to stop animal cruelty in future. 

Thanking you sir. 
Yours truly, 
Murugeswari s, 
HAWR - AWBI, 
SKGSG vice chairman, 
Madurai district, 
Tamil Nadu. 
9944942320. 
Skgsg2310@gmail.com

For the past few days, in Sankarankoil, Tenkasi district, municipal employees have taken away helpless stray dogs.  
          Seeing this, the animal welfare activist from the area Mr.  Subash Narayanan Cell No .  9043369798 has followed the vehicle and seen it.
 found more than 50 helpless stray dogs locked up in rooms inside the municipal garbage dump opposite the Sankaran Kovil new bus stand.
   Therefore, he lodged a complaint in Sankarankoil Nagar Police Station on 18.07.2023 at around 08.45 hours.  Then on the next day 19.07.2023 in the morning, he went to the municipal office to inquire about the condition of the caught dogs.  Some of the employees there, including the ward councilor, tried to attack Subash Narayanan.  Immediately after going to the police station, the police made her wait until the evening and threatened to file a case for preventing the municipal employees from working.
     When we inquired about this, it was revealed that there were legal violations in catching Stray Dogs in large numbers and performing surgeries and when Subash Narayanan found out and complained about it, thinking that there would be a problem, they released all the Stray Dogs that were not operated on in the area.  Therefore, I request you to conduct a proper investigation and find out the facts and take appropriate action against all those involved or accomplices in case of any wrongdoing.

Melakarunkulam village is located near Melapalayam in Tirunelveli district.  In the region where there are many agricultural plantations, dogs are bred for guarding purposes.

 In this case, it is said that dogs are missing from many houses since last night.

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam,
Recently I was watching YouTube vlogs of some people travelling to Nagaland. Surprisingly I came to know that in the meat markets of Dimapur as well as of Kohima, People are selling meat of stray dogs , rabbits and cats after catching and hunting them. The people in the meat market kills and cook these animals in front of the customers. 
One of the shop owner said that it is our culture.
I want to complain here that no culture can go against the law of the land.
Once Sati Pratha was a culture, also teen talak was a culture, both were abolished when law was enforced.
No one has right to catch hunt kill  cook and eat these animals that are protected by laws.
Even a famous Actor Salman Khan had to face court trial under hunting case.

complaint:
An incident of animal cruelty  perpetrated by an Indian Administrative Service officer posted in Punjab.
Details of the IAS Officer
Name Mr Keshav Hingonia 2012
Designation Special Secretary
Posting Location Department of Social Security And Women and Child Development,
Cadre Punjab
Abetment of Animal Cruelty Mr. Keshav, an IAS officer, has been persistently attempting to remove the puppies from the premises for several months. On 23rd May 2025, for the fourth time, the three puppies were relocated. I fed them approximately 930 pm, but by the following morning, they were missing, as of i am sending you this letter they havent been found. Even if Mr. Keshav Hingonia did not carry out the act of relocating the puppies himself, he has played a role in abetting this unlawful action by continuously inciting and pressuring others to remove them. It is important to note that under the Animal Birth Control Rules, 2023, no individual, Resident Welfare Association , or Estate
Management entity is legally permitted to relocate dogs. Such actions constitute a punishable offence under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, and Section 325 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. Additionally, the act of encouraging or provoking such illegal relocation falls under various provisions related to abetment of crime. Whatsapp chats, voice recording and video attached
Furthermore, there have been multiple incidents where the officer has wrongfully spread mis-information regarding laws, quoted fake case to prove his point, entered or encroached upon my space, constituting acts of trespass and misconduct. These actions reflect a clear abuse of authority and a sustained pattern of harassment that want serious attention and accountability. 
Misuse of Powers as IAS During 3rd time of relocation we had every possible proof of relocation and police helped us to locate and bring the puppies back. But in this case Mr. Keshav intervene and ask DSP Mullanpur Mohit Kumar Aggarwal PPS 97803 10501 shutdown the case, which he did and they never filled FIR under his influence. He indulged in politics while working as a civil servant, Mr. Keshav appointed him self as President of the ad-hoc committee list of Members attached. Then one day Vice President Chandraprabha
Gupta arranged a one on one meeting with me but it was preplanned by Mr. Keshav, and he started shouting and had a malicious intention to call me alone and tried to manhandle thats why i recorded the video of him shouting and he uses his position as IAS in every possible way to influence the police, people, and he thinks he is the law, you will notice in whatsapp messages sent by Mr. Keshav, Now after relocation he is even celebrating it like a victory.
Evidence I have WhatsApp group chats where Mr. Keshav talked about relocation of dogs and also encourage others to do so, to harass more provoking other residents by sending irrelevant videos, Calling out my name to defame me, I have whatsapp chat screenshots,video and voice recordings attached to this letter. where Mr. Keshav Hingonia is not willing to listen to or very aggressive because theres a law for animals in India. 
Prayer
In light of the grave nature of these allegations, which reflect a serious breach of conduct expected from an officer of the Indian Administrative Service, I respectfully request your esteemed office to
Take immediate cognizance of this complaint.
Initiate a thorough and impartial enquiry into the misconduct and misuse of power of the IAS Mr Keshav Hingonia.
Direct the appropriate authorities to take swift and stringent disciplinary action
against the officer if the allegations are found to be true, in accordance with the All India Services Conduct Rules, 1968, and relevant laws pertaining to animal welfare and protection of citizens.
place_of_incident:
Ambika Florence 
Ambika city, New Chandigarh, Mullanpur, Punjab 
140901

We, resident of village Jodhpur, Tehsil Abohar, District Fazilka, Punjab Pin Code: 152117; GPS: 30.131495, 74.374794, wish to raise a serious concern about 8-12 aggressive dogs regularly fed and sheltered by Mr Sarabjeet Singh and family.

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam
I Anurag Dhillon am resident of the above mentioned society and am feeding about 50-60 dogs everyday. I do my every bit for their welfare like taking them for treatments whenever needed, operating them from stop giving birth to new puppies and also run anti rabies campaign around my society. I have raised the question of creating feeding points around the society for these stray dogs in the RWA meetings in front of President and the offender Mr. Harcharan Grewal, but he bluntly said that stray/ community dogs are not our concern as we live in a gated society. I go from one place to another every evening to feed these animals and it takes me more than 2 hours everyday. I leave for work in morning and used to keep container for dog food at the entrance gate for vendor to come and deliver food. President of our society asked guard to remove the food from there and it was kept where people bring their pets for relieving purposes amidst pee and poops. The whole food was destroyed by ants and that days all 50-60 dogs were not given the food. I then started keeping container for 
food in the basement of our society within my own parking lot boundary but still President asked guards to remove the food for stray dogs and threatened to insult me in front of whole society by putting allegations that i was breaking rules. After a lot of resistance from my side i somehow still managed to keep the container there and delivery boy used to come there, deliver food in containers and leave. Now today president ordered guards to stop the delivery boy from entering the basement for food delivery. I even gave in writing that i take the full responsibility of these delivery guys and if they are found indulging in any illegal activity they will be stopped from entering the society and i will be responsible for their act. Still president Mr. Harcharan Grewal went on to blame me that i was creating problems and threated once again to slap charges of misconduct on me in front of whole society. As president it is his duty to pass instructions for making feeding points and to allot a place where food for these animals can be kept safe, but on the contrary he is creating problems for all the feeders and leave aside making feeding points he is not even allowing me to keep food for dogs at a safe place. He has appointed special guards and given them rods to beat the poor dogs out if by mistake they enter our society. A few months back an innocent stray dog was found dead in our society and her leg was broken in 2 pieces and there was no scratches on her body which clearly ahows that it was not an accident but was beaten and killed. A lot more is happening in our society on the orders of President as he openly said in Board meeting that I do not care about community dogs as we have a gated society. He even tried to force use of muzzle on one of the pets of a resident, as his dog accidentally scratched a girl. The dog is a beagle, a playful breed and it was just a minor accident but President made a 8-9 point notice mentioning force use of muzzle and relocating the dog as well as the resident. A lot more is happening sir/madam. I am doing everything on my own from feeding dogs to taking them to shelters to building small houses for them to operating them for birth control. Spend 2-3 hours every day and about 60-70 thousand every month for these lovely but poor stray dogs. Rather helping me in this noble cause Mr. President is trying his level best to stop me and discouraging all other feeders as well.
Kindly look into the matter at the earliest before others too feel threatened and leave taking care of these beautiful animals.

complaint:
I Dhruv Sethi, a resident of Sector 117, Mohali, Punjab, am writing to you regarding major atrocities against new born stray dogs and their mother in my gated society.

The mother dog lives here in the society since more than a year. She gave birth to 9 healthy puppies. The society management and the builders of the society got to know about the situation and after around a month, they forcefully threw 7 new born puppies along with their mother into the nearby fields to let them die starving.

2 puppies were kept safe by a resident of our society, in their home but 7 of them were thrown out.

The stray dogs kept on hunting the new borns and injured the mother dog due to which she came back to the society to hide in shadow. The stray dogs outside started hunting the puppies and ultimately killed 5 puppies which were already suffering from starvation.

Only 2 of them survived, so I brought them back to the society so that I can keep them safe until they grow for some more months and get stronger to survive the nature.

BUT, the society management is harrassing us and took the remaining 4 puppies and their mother under the radar and are willing to throw them out at even far place.

We need your support in order to save the lives of remaining 4 innocent puppies and their mother because they have already seen a lot.

The Management and the Builders are on a rampage to remove them from the society and will attack them whenever they see them roaming.

complaint:
Dear Sir/Madam,

This is the complaint regarding Unethical, Illegal Veterinary Practices and Professionally irresponsible practices being conducted at Dr. Amit Doon Pet Clinics in Dehradun routinely.
This practitioner is running a surgical training program on monthly basis under Small Animal Research And Learning internship program. Last month I contacted Dr. Amit after seeing an advertisement on Facebook regarding surgical training program for spay-neuter procedures. I joined their program as a keen learner but soon realized that they are doing unethical activities on their clinic premises and they do not have any facilities for the training program. In fact they are using this program just for the monetary benefits at the cost of health of speechless beings.

As a veterinary professional with good professional standing, I suggested Dr. Amit multiple times about this neglect but instead of considering my suggestion, he pushed me to take part in his ill practices and threatened me to face legal consequences if i refuse to continue with him. After this, I terminated my internship with him and decided to submit this complaint as per my professional obligation.
The specific instances of misconduct are as follows:
1. Unethical Drug Practices: I was routinely pressured to recommend and perform over-prescription of drugs, and to engage in the dangerous practice of mixing various drugs to save on the cost of syringes. This poses a severe health risk to the animals and is a gross violation of medical standards.
2. Capturing stray animals to provide surgeries.
3. Use of Unsterilized Instruments: During surgeries, they are using unsterilized surgical instruments. This is a severe act of medical negligence that could lead to life-threatening infections and suffering for the animals.

I urge the Animal Welfare Board of India to take immediate and serious action against Dr. Amit Singh Bisht, to prevent them from causing further harm to animals and from exploiting and intimidating young professionals.

Thank you for your time and for your commitment to animal welfare.
place_of_incident: Dr. Amit Doon Pet Life Clinics, near Dilaram Gurudwara

complainant_name_org: Stray Dogs Sterilization Centre Cum Hospital
complaint: That the Centre is maintained in unhygienic and inhumane conditions, with dogs kept in a deplorable state. The floors were covered with urine and faecal matter, the kennels reeked of foul odour, and the entire premises were unfit for any living being, reflecting utter neglect and lack of due care. The Centre lacked adequate food, with only a small quantity of rice and turmeric available. The dogs were neither fed timely nor given a proper diet, leaving them malnourished, emaciated, and in visibly poor health, amounting to clear cruelty and neglect. The Centre was found in complete darkness, with no electricity, bulbs, or fans functioning. The dogs were left to suffer in suffocating, foul, and uninhabitable conditions, amounting to gross negligence and clear violation of the Animal Birth Control Rules and the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. That the staff were illegally capturing dogs and failed to maintain mandatory records under the ABC Rules, 2023. The veterinarian, Dr. Rajeev, admitted that no records existed, claiming he relied on memory, thereby exposing the dogs to illegal relocation and untraceable loss, in gross violation of law. That skeletal remains of dogs were found buried in the greenery surrounding the Centre, raising grave suspicion of illegal killings and unlawful activities, in blatant violation of animal welfare laws. That an aged dog was found abandoned in a washroom on the second floor, lying in a near-death state with an e-collar, while the staff feigned ignorance of its presence, evidencing wilful cruelty and gross neglect. That removed reproductive organs of dogs were found stored in a plastic paint bucket, in gross violation of the ABC Rules, 2023, veterinary protocols, and biomedical waste norms. Such unhygienic handling poses grave risks of infection and disease, amounting to cruelty and punishable offences under the PCA Act, 1960 and Bio-Medical Waste Management Rules, 2016. It was further found that Dr. Rajeev conducted sterilizations at night, which is strictly prohibited. That two goats were found unlawfully confined within the Centre, which is authorised solely for dogs. This reflects blatant misuse of the facility, diversion of resources, and violations of the ABC Rules, 2023 and the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960. That earlier visits recorded about 150 dogs at the Centre, but only 116 were found on a recent inspection. The veterinary officer vaguely claimed 106 were to be released and 10 had complaints, with no records to verify. Such discrepancies raise grave concerns of mismanagement, illegal relocation, and violations of the ABC Rules, 2023 and the PCA Act, 1960. That despite the Honourable Supreme Court order dated 22.08.2025 directing release of all apprehended dogs, Dr. Rajeev wilfully failed and refused to comply, thereby unlawfully detaining the animals in defiance of judicial mandate. That these acts violate the ABC Rules, 2023, the PCA Act, 1960, and the fundamental duty under Article 51A g of the Constitution. I request to the authorities to Conduct an immediate inspection of the respective ABC Centre under Rule 6 of the ABC Rules, 2023, 	Take necessary action, including cancellation of the recognition of the said centre and permanently shut down the centre and 	Direct the concerned State/Local Authorities to ensure compliance with law and protection of the dogs.
place_of_incident: Stray Dogs Sterilization Centre Cum Hospital, Sector 27, North District, Rohini, Delhi - 110085

complaint:

Illegal relocation: Sterilised/vaccinated dogs, by law, must be returned to their location. Current drives permanently displace them without lawful cause.
Cruel handling: Dogs caught with excessive force, confined in unsafe vehicles, causing injury/distress.
Deaths/suffering: Many die or are abandoned without food/water/shelter, violating PCA Act Sec 11
gnoring ABC Rules: Removal only allowed for sterilisation, vaccination, or rabies/terminal illness, criteria ignored.
State-sponsored cruelty: Government-led, wilful defiance of statutory duty under PCA Act Sec 3

Relief Sought:
Immediate halt to removals.
AWBI directives to all state or municipal bodies for strict PCA and ABC compliance.
Urgent AWBI inspection with public report.
Prosecution under PCA Act Sec 11 and 38 against responsible officials.

complaint:
I am writing to raise an urgent concern regarding the relocation of a stray dog to Basement B2 Parking No MB2944 allocated to Flat No A8 Tower 10 Type 4 in Kidwai Nagar East

As per the current animal welfare regulations relocating community dogs is not permitted except for temporary purposes such as sterilization or vaccination Despite this the dog was moved to the basement around three months ago by a resident The basement is an enclosed poorly lit and fully cemented area which is unsuitable for the dogs health and wellbeing Stray dogs require access to open spaces and natural surroundings

complaint:
A dog on our campus, named Archie, has been missing from the University premises for the last 4-5 days. We have tried looking for him everywhere, and he is not to be found. There have been multiple attempts by the University administration to try and relocate him somewhere outside campus, but we had till now been able to prevent that. However, during vacations, when students are back at their home, we believe the university administration has secretly relocated him to a place we believe to be Bahadurgarh, Haryana. 

Archie has been born and brought up on campus, and survival for him at such a remote location will be extremely difficult to nearly impossible. While we have received multiple complaints against his behaviour, we have taken steps like leashing him during certain hours and also giving him calming medicines to sooth his behaviour. On this basis, we got surety from the administration that they would not relocate Archie.

However, to our utter disbelief, they did exactly what that promised against and have dropped him off. We have tried get information from the University but everyone has been denying any knowledge of such relocation itself. We believe he has in fact been relocated and would not have left the campus vicinity off of his own accord.

We further believe that being a law school, such utter disregard of the law against relocation of stray dogs deserves to be strictly reprimanded.

We hope the AWBI can help us in this, and get proof that Archie has in fact been relocated and that steps be taken against the university regarding the same.

Attached are photos of Archie for identification purposes. We, however, possess no photographic proof of his relocation and require your assistance in investigation of the same.

complaint:
A man in his late 50s or 60s, driving Tata Punch, DL14C0997, said on MCD Water House, drove over a 11 plus old street dog that everyone in the colony feeds, and dragged him for over 100 Meters under his car, in hopes that he could get over the dog and run away.
I found the dog under the axel of his car bleeding through his mouth and nose and with much shouting on him the man got off the car and looked unfazed. I asked him to take out the jack and pull up the axel to which he replied that that car does not have a jack. He then went off somewhere to get bricks or something to which I noticed the car moving, realised that even the handbreak wasnt pulled up.
We somehow had him lift up the car using bars of wood. Upon hearing from a witness who saw the whole thing from her balcony, said, he dragged the dog under the car and raced too hard to get past the dog.
When I confronted him and asked him to take the dog to the hospital, he said Haldi Milk will work. Additionally he asked me to talk to him with respect WHEN the dog is still under his car and did not budge and repeated to call him with respect. I had no plans for that.
Having had the dog have milk and water. I asked him again to take the dog to the vet, he said he would but this wasnt his fault and that he has raised him all this long but this was honestly the first time I saw this man ever in my life. Having to have said that he raised the dog does not give him the liberty to kill him too. I have seen the dog for 11 years now, taken him to the vet multiple times when he or ANY other dog in the lane or the area broke their leg or for injections of rabies etc. or even when a puppy dies, having them cremated properly. 
I have been living in the mentioned house for 11 years now and have seen the dog grow up and everyone on this street knows by now that with over 13 dogs on this lane alone, all grown up, that they sit under, over or in front of the car and drive slowly and check before driving off that the dogs have went away.
He further went on to blame me on how I wasnt there when other dogs died under cars and am all over him over this one and refused to accept what he did was still wrong. I threatened to lodge a FIR if he did not provide me with bills of his checkups.
In the end, he did not take the dog to the vet saying the dog did not budge or got on the car when I enquired with a girl. She mentioned how she was in the vet shop with the man for medicine prescription.
The dog still needs to be presented to he vet for the doctor to examine for an internal damages, apart from his visible scratches all over his body, legs, chest, a opened and bleeding nose, mouth and head that has been scratched and split open along with his nose.
I am taking an initiative to take him to he vet after I lodge this complaint.

complaint: We are seeking your kind attention to the cruel behavior of residents of our colony towards innocent creatures. Residents of B19, Manak Vihar, P.O. Tilak Nagar, New Delhi 110018, Sahwney, Himmat Singh from A37, Mr. Mehta B19, and others are engaged in throwing the pillows, feeding bowels of dogs, taking off the coats offered to them in addition to hitting them and throwing water at the dogs asleep. They harassed us for feeding the dogs in our area with the notion that these animals bite several people on the way and they must be relocated to some other places to ensure their security. They carry the long sticks with them to scare away the dogs hit them for no reason and tease the sleeping dogs, apart from the kids pelting stones at them. We were called off by the RWA meeting being the only female in the absence of any male among 25 other males, many of which had nothing to do more than agitating irrelevant matters and we are compelled to keep the stray dogs inside our house, with the already existing 3 adopted dogs or throw it at some local hospital. Besides that the two dogs are being hit whenever we release them intermittently as per the instructions of our RWA which the local builders and touts of our colony patronize. They have been a constant threat to our security ever since. They also include our neighbors who are indulged in illegal commercial activities at A21, B13, B26, and B19 residences and other local touts who have a history of relocating and poisoning dogs. Maam volunteers sterilize and vaccinate the dogs in our area regularly. I urge that no animal should be harmed by any of the residents in our area ensuring the safety of dogs. Strict action should be taken against those who are keen on relocating them to unfamiliar sites where the survival of relocated dogs would be difficult. I have enclosed the names and contact numbers of our RWA Chairperson and board members for your perusal. Above all I hope you read this and help restore the sanity and security of our furry friends. Thank you for lending us your ear.

complaint:
Two dogs, the first named Chichi, light brown, female with slight black around her nose and tail, and the second a black male dog were caught from around dwarka sec 14- nlu delhi area and relocated somewhere far from their territory so that they cannot find their way back. This violates the provisions of 
Sec 11 part 1 i PCA
Rule 7, 3, 4, 5, 6 ABC rules 2001
The Stray dogs management rules 2001 and 
Sec 325 BNS

Please take action and request the Registrar to share information on the current location of the two dogs.

complaint:
I,Ritu chugh was coming back from the d block market after visiting Balaji mandir and picking up my daughter from tuition.On my way back to home some dogs started following us and out of love and affection my daughter fed them something she was eating and on seeing this the residents of D-75 Vivek Vihar phase 1 started to qurrel with me and lady from top floor threatened me that I will not feed these dogs there and if I do then I will be responsible for whatever happens.
Next day that is on 10 september when I went through the gully of the aforementioned house I was prepared for confrontation and started recording video and asked my daughter to do the same.Upon reaching one lady and one man came down and started throwing vinegar on the dogs I was feeding and beating them with danda.I have video proof of this and they even threw vinegar on me.
From next day changed the spot of feeding those dogs to avoid confrontation and so that those dogs dont have to go through this cruelty again.
Then 4 days ago that is 25 September I was coming after feeding those dogs and they were following me , so I closed the gate behind me so that they would not reach the area they were beaten in and the next day that is 26 September those same people confronted me and started harassing me again.
Next day that is 27 September I took my husband along with me to show what was happening with me on feeding the dogs and one man from that house started abusing me and my husband both and he got so aggressive that he had to be held by other people or else he would have hit my husband and we have witness for this as well
Now we fear that if we dont take a legal action against them then they will poison the dogs and kill them and they have even threatened us by saying that they dare us cross that gully.
I sincerely request to look into this matter with urgency as the life of these dogs is in danger.

complaint:
Mohit kumar
House no 402/c, F-block, Gali no 21, Badarpur Border, 3rd 60 feet road, Delhi, 110044
16th August 2024

To,
The Secretary,
Animal Welfare Board of India,
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying,
Government of India,
NIAW Campus, 42 Mile Stone, Delhi-Agra Highway,
NH-2, Ballabhgarh, Haryana - 121004

Subject: Urgent Complaint Regarding Cruelty Against Street Puppies in My Area

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to bring to your immediate attention a grave issue of animal cruelty occurring in my neighborhood, located at Badarpur Border, 3rd 60 feet road, Delhi, 110044.  I am a college student, and I have been taking care of three street puppies in my area. However, certain residents of my neighborhood have been inflicting severe harm on these innocent animals.

Over the past few days, I have witnessed these neighbors brutally attacking the puppies whenever they sit near their house. In the last two days alone, two of the puppies have had their legs broken due to the beatings they have received. This situation has left the puppies in immense pain and suffering, and I am deeply concerned for their safety.

Despite my inability to visit the police station to file a formal complaint, I am determined to seek justice for these animals. I have photographic evidence of the injuries sustained by the puppies, which I am ready to share with your esteemed organization.

Given the seriousness of the situation, I kindly request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take the following actions:

Investigation: Conduct an urgent investigation into the matter and gather evidence of the cruelty being inflicted on these puppies.
Legal Action: Initiate legal proceedings against the individuals responsible, in accordance with the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.
Medical Assistance: Provide immediate medical care to the injured puppies.
Awareness Campaign: Educate the community about the importance of treating animals with compassion and the legal consequences of animal cruelty.
I trust that your organization will take swift and decisive action to protect these innocent animals and prevent further cruelty. I am willing to provide any additional information or assistance required to support this cause.

Thank you for your attention and prompt action in this matter.

Yours sincerely,
Mohit kumar

complaint:
I am filing this complaint to report repeated and brutal cruelty towards community dogs inside Manipal University, Jaipur campus. Multiple incidents have taken place where security guards have physically beaten dogs mercilessly, resulting in severe injuries. One recent incident involves a dog who was beaten so badly that its leg is fractured and it has a deep open wound. This is not an isolated event several such acts of violence towards friendly campus dogs have occurred repeatedly and have been recorded by students.
These dogs are harmless, friendly, and well-accepted among students. They do not display aggression and coexist peacefully here. Instead of managing them humanely, security guards have intentionally beaten, injured and chased them, causing pain, disability and trauma. This is a serious violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 and ABC Rules 2023, both of which protect community animals from relocation, abuse and harm. No dog may be beaten, removed, or injured by campus authorities, and feeding/caring for them cannot be restricted as per law.
Students have video evidence of the abuse, medical proof of injuries and multiple recorded incidents of guards attacking dogs intentionally. I am attaching visual proof along with this complaint.
I request AWBI to take immediate and strict action by investigating the incidents of cruelty and identifying those responsible, issuing legal notice to the university and the security personnel involved, ensuring that the injured dogs receive medical treatment and are allowed to remain safely on campus, enforcing the PCA Act and ABC Rules to prevent any further abuse, and mandating the establishment of monitoring systems and designated feeding zones so that these community dogs can coexist peacefully with students. These animals cannot speak for themselves they are innocent, suffering, and urgently need protection before more lives are harmed.
place_of_incident: Manipal University , Jaipur-Ajmer Express Highway, Dehmi Kalan, Near GVK Toll Plaza, Jaipur

This person, Ashok Kumar Dada and his and his son Yogesh Dada are casing distress and issues among the street dogs. We have also treated 2 dogs for injuries related to sticks.

Causing harm to street dogs and distress.

Spreading misinformation towards the dogs.

complaint: On 23 January 2025, at ward no. 22, Khairthal, as per the order of the District Collector, stray dogs were picked up from various locations within the ward by the Nagar Parishad officials in a pick-up vehicle without a number plate or license plate. The vehicle did not even look designed specifically for animal pick-up and handling. How the Nagar Parishad officials handled the dogs was extremely inhumane and in violation of the Honourable Supreme Court guidelines regarding treating stray animals. The dogs were brutally kicked and struck with sticks during their capture, as documented in the videos and photographs attached to this complaint. Additionally, as per a newspaper report attached, the Collector has stated that the dogs are to be relocated to a shelter outside the town. This raises serious concerns as it appears inconsistent with the Supreme Court directive, which mandates that stray dogs must be vaccinated, sterilized, and returned to their original locations. In the past, there have been reports of dogs being abandoned in jungles, even when they posed no threat. I urge you to look into this matter urgently and ensure the humane treatment and safe return of these innocent animals following the law. Thank you for your attention. I look forward to a prompt response.

complaint:
I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to bring to your attention a matter of urgent concern regarding the relocation of dogs from certain societies and the potential threat to their lives.

Recently, it has come to our attention that in several areas, there has been a movement to relocate dogs from residential societies. While we understand the need for managing populations and addressing concerns related to animal welfare and safety, we are deeply troubled by reports suggesting that some individuals or groups are advocating for the removal of all dogs from their respective societies, with threats of harming or killing these animals if they are not removed promptly.

As an organization committed to animal welfare and the protection of all sentient beings, we believe that such actions are not only inhumane but also unlawful. Dogs, like all animals, have inherent rights to live free from harm and cruelty. Threatening their lives or forcibly removing them from their habitats without proper consideration for their well-being is a violation of these rights.

We urge you to intervene in this matter promptly to ensure that the rights and welfare of these dogs are protected. We request your assistance in investigating these reports and taking appropriate action to prevent any harm to the animals in question. Additionally, we encourage dialogue and collaboration with local authorities and community members to find humane and sustainable solutions to issues related to stray animal populations.

Furthermore, we would appreciate it if you could provide us with updates on any developments regarding this issue and inform us of any measures being taken to address it. Our organization is willing to offer support and assistance in any way possible to ensure the safety and well-being of these animals.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. We look forward to your prompt response and cooperation in safeguarding the rights of these vulnerable creatures.

This action is unlawful since the ABC Rules 2023 mandate sterilization vaccination and release back to the same location it is in contempt of court since the Supreme Court has ordered that dogs must not be displaced and it is cruel and dangerous since relocation disorients dogs spreads disease and increases the risk of dog bites in the new areas. Who will take responsibility if relocated unmonitored dogs attack residents in new locations The liability rests on the District Commissioner Ravindra PN and the municipal officers including Amrutha who authorized this illegal act. I request AWBI to take immediate cognizance ensure that this relocation is stopped without delay direct that all dogs already removed must be brought back to their original territories and enforce strict compliance with CNVR as per law.

We need your immediate help.

complaint:
I am writing with deep concern regarding an incident in our residential society involving the unauthorized and illegal removal of 9 - 12 community dogs. and in past also they kept doing such cruelty. It includes 1 monther dog as well who was nursing her 5 puppies and now puppies survival also is at risk without mother.

As per our findings, 3 members of the Management Committee - Soumitra, Rajeshwari, and Akash - had encouraged all residents to sign a petition to request an official dog sterilization and vaccination drive through the municipal body Purushabha. However, what followed was not an official municipal operation - confirmed by the Purishabha officer. 

Instead, with the help of unofficial channels and potentially misusing the residents trust, they arranged for dog catchers - who were not from any authorized agency - to remove these animals. We have some clues suggesting these individuals were paid 500 INR per dog, and some dogs may have been relocated or sent to slaughterhouses. This was falsely communicated to residents as a municipal sterilization drive, which is a serious violation of law and amounts to animal cruelty. 

This covert and misleading action is not only unethical but also potentially criminal. Under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, and various rulings by the Honble Supreme Court and High Courts, stray dogs cannot be relocated or killed - only sterilized and vaccinated as per AWBI and local body guidelines. We urgently request your help in the following:
Immediate investigation into this illegal activity
Verification of whether this was an officially sanctioned municipal operation
Recovery and safety assurance for the missing dogs
Strict action against those involved in organizing illegal dog removal/killing

Without your support, we fear that more dogs in our society will be harmed or lost. These dogs were healthy, community-fed, vaccinated also and part of our eco-system. They deserved better. 

Thank you for your attention and for standing up for voiceless lives.
place_of_incident:
Abhee Prakruthi Villa
Sy No. 46/1 & 46/2, Iggalur Town Chandapura Circle, Anekal, Taluk, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560099

complaint:
I am writing this letter with deep pain and frustration to raise an urgent and serious complaint regarding the reckless and intentional killing of community dogs by lorries and precast vehicles operating in the Sobha construction area.
Over the last 10 months, I have tragically lost 10 plus dogs, most of them innocent puppies. These dogs lived either in the construction area or near the adjoining roads and were being looked after with care. Sadly, the lorry drivers in the Sobha Precast zone have shown shocking cruelty and carelessness, crushing the animals to death without remorse or accountability.
Despite raising this issue several times with Sobha Precast management, we have received no resolution. The managers continuously redirect us to each other, wasting our time and avoiding any action. This cycle of negligence has only emboldened the drivers and worsened the situation. In fact, dogs killed on-site are buried without any notification to the feeders or caretakers.
I hereby request your immediate intervention and support, and would like to place the following demands:
	1.	File a formal complaint against Sobha Builders and their lorry/construction vehicle drivers for animal cruelty and repeated killing of community dogs.
	2.	Mandate installation of CCTV cameras at all major points of the Sobha construction site and roads leading in and out of the area, to monitor vehicle behavior and animal safety.
	3.	Set up official billboards along these roads to raise awareness on:
                a.     Legal protection for street animals
	        b. 	Dog feeding rights
	        c. 	Animal accident prevention
	        d.	Strict prohibition of overspeeding
	4.	Enforce a monthly driving awareness session for all drivers entering the Sobha Precast construction site, specifically covering animal safety and legal consequences of roadkill.
Please take this issue with the urgency and seriousness it deserves. These dogs are voiceless beings who trusted us, and they deserve safety, not brutal deaths. I am doing my part to care for them, but I cannot protect them from high-speed trucks unless this system changes.
Thank you for your time and support. I hope to hear from your team soon regarding the steps being taken.

complaint:
I am writing to formally report ongoing harassment faced by me and my feeder near the Sobha Dream Acres residential area. Over the past few weeks, we have been consistently confronted by individuals who demand that we stop feeding community dogs, citing concerns about biting incidents.
I would like to clarify that feeding and aggression are not directly related. Since we started feeding the dogs regularly, their aggression has significantly reduced. With consistent feeding and care, the situation has improved. Therefore, it is unfair and unjustified to harass those who are trying to help these animals.

Last week, an alarming incident occurred where one of our feeders was threatened with a knife by a man who demanded we stop feeding the dogs. This is a serious safety concern, and such behavior cannot be tolerated. Unfortunately, the Sobha society has not designated any official feeding zones for the community dogs, which leaves us with no choice but to feed in areas with less pedestrian traffic early in the morning. Despite our efforts to avoid inconveniencing residents, we are still subjected to daily verbal abuse and threats, both from within the society and from nearby residents.
Therefore, I would like to request the following:
1. Immediate intervention to address this harassment and ensure the safety of feeders. 
2. A formal complaint to be lodged on our behalf to the local authorities, if applicable. 
3. Install a billboard in our area outlining community dog feeding guidelines and legal protections. 
4. Issue a certified feeder ID card or certificate from your organization for all active feeders in our group.
We are committed to the ethical treatment and care of community animals and need your urgent support in continuing this work safely and respectfully.

complaint:
It has come to attention that certain residents known for their animosity towards community dogs have united to harass animal caretakers and are now openly advocating for the relocation of these dogs in public forums. Such actions not only reflect a blatant disregard for animal welfare but also constitute illegal activities under Indian law.

Legal Protections for Community Dogs

Animal Birth Control Dogs Rules 2001 Enacted under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960, these rules mandate the sterilization and vaccination of street dogs as humane methods for population control. Importantly, they prohibit the relocation of street dogs from their territories pib.gov.in.

Supreme Court Directives The Supreme Court of India has, in various orders, explicitly prohibited the relocation of dogs, emphasizing that such actions are not permissible iitrpr.ac.in.

Indian Penal Code Sections 428 and 429 These sections impose severe penalties, including imprisonment of up to five years, for acts of cruelty towards animals, encompassing maiming or causing harm iitrpr.ac.in.

Implications of Unauthorized Relocation

Relocating community dogs disrupts the established balance within the ecosystem and can lead to unforeseen consequences, such as the introduction of unsterilized and unvaccinated dogs into the area, thereby exacerbating the issues residents aim to resolve.

Call to Action

It is imperative for all residents to adhere to the legal frameworks in place and collaborate with animal welfare organizations to implement humane and lawful measures for managing community dogs. Engaging in illegal activities, including the harassment of animal caretakers and unauthorized relocation of dogs, will result in legal action and penalties as prescribed by law.

Promoting a community culture that respects and protects animal rights is not only a legal obligation but also a reflection of our societal values.

complaint:
I and other feeders are nearing both mental and physical collapse as we continue to deal with the distressing situations. As per the ABC Rules 2023, I am now appealing to the Karnataka Animal Welfare Board under the direct supervision of the Animal Welfare Board of India, for a fair assessment and resolution as per the relevant laws of this country. 

Please allow me to outline the key points of concern:

- Exhaustion from Harassment: The constant harassment, both direct and indirect, has taken a severe toll on my mental and physical well-being. The ongoing stress is unbearable, and I am being targeted and punished for trying to do the right thing for these defenseless animals.

- Clarification on the core accusation: I want to make it abundantly clear that I have and can NOT relocated any dog into the Suncity apartment complex. Being in this field since 2007 i have personally witnessed the horrors of dog territorial fights among dogs, and I would never, under any circumstance, encourage or facilitate such a situation. I understand the potential danger and suffering it causes, and I would be the one who would have to manage the consequences.

- BBMP, SPCA and Bellandur Police Failure to Assess the Situation Fairly: The failure of BBMP, SPCA and the Bellandur Police to properly assess this situation in a fair and just manner has resulted in the continued harassment of both the dogs and the feeders. Their lack of action has led to an environment where innocent lives have been put at risk. I cannot, in good conscience, allow these defenseless animals to be condemned to an unjust fate. 

- Request for an Independent Conversation: I request that you arrange for an independent conversation with me, where I can provide you with the true ground-level understanding of the situation. It is critical that you fully grasp the reality of what is happening, as the current narrative being propagated is deeply misleading and harmful.

- Living Conditions for the Dogs: As a result of the failure to address this issue appropriately by Authorities, I have been left with no other option to fill my flat with discarded resident dogs - who lived with us since ages and now their place had been snatched - in a desperate attempt to keep them alive. This situation is untenable. It is neither acceptable nor appropriate for the dogs nor for myself to be subjected to such living conditions. This is a temporary measure, but it is not a sustainable or humane solution.

In conclusion, I am at my wits end, and the situation cannot continue in its current form. I implore you to take immediate action, engage with me directly to understand the reality of this issue, and ensure that a fair and compassionate resolution is reached. The lives of these animals and the well-being of those who care for them depend on it.

Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter. I look forward to your response.

Sincerely,
Minu Singh
place_of_incident: Flat No. 107, Block 6, Sun City Apartments, Sarjapur Outer Ring Road Junction, Bengaluru

complaint:
This complaint report is being submitted to highlight the unlawful relocation of community dogs residing within our apartment premises and the harassment faced by feeders and animal caretakers. Despite repeated appeals to the relevant authorities, including the police and the BBMP, there has been no effective intervention, and the Resident Welfare Association RWA continues to act in violation of the Animal Welfare Board of India AWBI guidelines.

Details of the Complaint

Community dogs that were residing peacefully within the apartment premises have been forcibly relocated outside the premises. This action directly violates the guidelines issued by the AWBI, which clearly state that community dogs cannot be displaced from their territory. Such forced removal puts the dogs at extreme risk, exposing them to territorial fights, starvation, and road accidents.

Large groups of residents were mobilized to intimidate and harass individuals who were feeding and caring for these animals. The feeders were subjected to verbal abuse, threats, and hostile confrontations by these mobs. Despite police intervention being sought, no action was taken against those harassing the feeders.

The police, despite being present during incidents of harassment, failed to take any steps to protect the feeders and caretakers. Instead of upholding the AWBI guidelines, the police and BBMP officials have shown a bias toward the RWA, enabling them to continue their unlawful actions. The lack of accountability and enforcement of laws has emboldened the RWA to act with impunity.

Individuals who speak up for the dogs and their rights are being subjected to constant monitoring. Residents have been clicking photos and recording videos of animal welfare activists and feeders, invading their privacy and attempting to intimidate them. This targeted harassment has created an unsafe environment for those advocating for animal welfare.

Some of the relocated dogs are either senior dogs, disabled, or vulnerable, making it impossible for them to fend for themselves. By forcing these animals out, the RWA has knowingly put them in life-threatening situations. These actions constitute cruelty and are punishable under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960.

Following the last notice from AWBI, an Animal Welfare Association was established. Despite this, no consultation was made with this body before taking decisions regarding the community dogs. The RWA continues to act unilaterally without considering ethical and legal obligations.

Relief and Action Requested

In light of the above-mentioned violations and harassment, we request the following immediate actions. Immediate intervention by the concerned authorities to ensure the safe return of the displaced community dogs to their original location. Strict action against the individuals and groups responsible for the unlawful removal of dogs and harassment of feeders. Clear directives to the police and BBMP officials to enforce AWBI guidelines and take necessary action against violators. Assurance of protection for animal feeders and caretakers from any further harassment. Recognition and active involvement of the newly formed Animal Welfare Association in all future decisions regarding community animals. Awareness programs for the RWA and residents on the legal and ethical aspects of coexisting with community dogs.

Conclusion

The continued harassment of animal caretakers and the illegal relocation of dogs are serious offenses that require immediate redressal. We urge the authorities to take swift action in addressing these concerns and ensuring that AWBI guidelines and Indian laws are upheld.

We look forward to a prompt response and necessary action on this matter.

complaint:
I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding an incident of animal cruelty that I witnessed on 27/02/2025 at 8:30 PM at Shop 1, Site no 24, Horamavu Agara Main Rd, Horamavu Agara, Horamavu, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560043. Employees of Thatha Tea were involved in an act of extreme cruelty against a helpless street dog. They were hitting the dog on the head and striking it with a hard object, causing severe distress and potential injury to the animal.

When I intervened and confronted them about their inhumane actions, they dismissed my concerns, stating that I should not be concerned about street dogs and that it was none of my business. Additionally, they asked me to lower my voice instead of acknowledging their wrongdoing. Such actions reflect a blatant disregard for animal welfare laws and ethical treatment of stray animals.

This act of cruelty is in direct violation of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, which prohibits causing unnecessary pain and suffering to animals. As per the Act, those responsible for harming animals must be held accountable for their actions. I request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take immediate action against those responsible and ensure that such incidents do not occur in the future.

I urge you to:

Investigate the matter and identify the individuals responsible for harming the street dog.

Take appropriate legal action against them under the relevant provisions of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.
