complaint:
The accused is my neighbor. This complaint is lodged by Chelsea Panchal under the guardianship of Neeru Panchal, currently residing at 555A, Sec-2, Bahadurgarh, Haryana. The accused is the resident of sector 2 as well, currently living on rent nearby; their plot resides directly facing our house which is house no. 608, which now they are currently building. First situation: During late winter, the accused was bitten by a street dog. The reason was not clear why the dog bit her, but as soon as the dog bit her, the dog ran with its tail tucked under its legs. No one in the area knew why the dog bit her. Some of the dogs here are neutered and vaccinated as per the old residents. The accused was again bitten by another dog of the area again for no apparent reason, as per her statement; like the previous time, she blamed us for giving food to them, which is less of a concern. But as per her response to the first situation, she started carrying a thick piece of wood to scare them in her defense and started hitting them with small stones. In response to the second situation, she called her nephew, who is a minor and throws bricks and beats every dog with thick wooden sticks they find in their way. The accused has gotten an oral warning from our side many times to immediately discontinue this act of violence towards the street dogs, but the accused person is hiding behind her nephew; we have tried talking to each member of their family, and each of them thinks that this act of violence is right. The concern is that they follow an act of violence each time they see any dog on the street. Right now they abuse them when they have not fully shifted in their under-construction house. What will happen when they permanently reside here? According to the accused, two different dogs bit her, for no clear reason, as per her. All the dogs here have lived here before us, and to abuse them and to kick them out of their territory is an unlawful act. We have lived here for almost 2.5 years, and hundreds of unknown Swiggy, Zomato, and Blinkit people have come in front of our house; none of them have once gotten bitten. The children of our area also play with them; the dogs never showed any aggression or even growled. The point is that the accused is blaming the street dogs and performing an act of violence towards them directly and indirectly. Each time we sit in our house and they come to check their plot, we hear voices of dogs being hit with heavy objects. The dogs they hit were still adult dogs 2.5 years ago when we came into this neighborhood, unaware of their age, but the neighbors approximation is that the dogs have entered the senior dog category. The dogs are not aggressive yet, but if they keep on hitting them, the day is not very far off that they become aggressive.
Hence, the people in the area suggested the accused give street dogs something to eat or told her to carry a big stick to scare them off. She never tried offering them food or anything but straightforwardly opted for violence in response to the second situation. None of the neighbors or any person living nearby suggested her to actually beat them or forcefully push them out from anywhere near her house.
I am traumatized to see such actions of the accused; the act of violence by her and her nephew are outrageous and affect the essence of the society deeply. I beg the AWBI to take measurable and righteous actions so them beings do not suffer any longer violence from the accused in her actions of revenge or grudges she held from the above two situations.
place_of_incident: H.NO-608 ,SECTOR-2 ,BAHADURGARH ,HARYANA

complaint:
I am writing to complaint about two residents of Ireo The Corridors Society in sector 67A of Gurgaon. Ms Seema moved into the society on 19 October 2023 and have been feeding some community dogs since the past 10 months. On several occasions she have been harassed and taunted by some residents for feeding and have been told that if you stop feeding them then they will either leave the society or die of hunger. This did not deter her from continuing my feeding drive along with some other residents.

However, on Sunday 10 November at around 9 30 pm when she was feeding the dogs Mr. Yuvraj mobile no. 9871136622 a resident of A5-102 tower starting making a video of her feeding the dogs. she informed him that making a video like this without her consent is illegal to which he replied most rudely that he was only filming the dogs. complaint:
Dear Sir,

I am Monika Mittal, a resident of CHD Avenue 71, Sector 71, Gurgaon. I am writing to bring to your immediate attention a deeply concerning incident that took place in our society regarding the illegal relocation or possible killing of a brown community dog.

There have been multiple cases of animal abuse and cruelty in our society, as reported in my earlier complaint, no. .9-7/2024-25/906/PCA. Unfortunately, these incidents have led to increased dog bites reported by some residents. Using these complaints as an excuse, certain RWA members have begun forcefully relocating or possibly killing dogs within and outside the society, assaulting us whenever we tried to educate them that the dogs picked up were not involved in any of the reported incidents.

On 25th August, we received reports from several society members that a cage was placed in the basement near Tower 11 to catch this particular brown dog. The feeders were prevented from feeding him, leaving the dog hungry and desperate. The dog, driven by hunger, went into the cage and was caught. Later, an unknown van took this dog to an undisclosed location, or worse, killed him. These RWA members have openly threatened us and the MCG team, stating that they would kill the dog if caught.

This incident occurred near the Tower 11 ramp towards the basement, where there are cameras installed. We have requested the Estate Office to share the CCTV footage from 25th August onwards, but they are not cooperating. The CCTV footage is active only for 30 days, so it is urgent and critical to retrieve this footage to track the missing dog, if alive.

We urgently request your intervention to help us obtain the CCTV footage and investigate the whereabouts of the missing dog. This situation is time-sensitive, and the safety of the dog is at stake.

Please treat this matter with the urgency it deserves.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this distressing situation.

Sincerely,
Monika Mittal

complaint:
Respected Sir/Madam,
I wish to file a formal complaint against the arbitrary and unlawful actions of the management of Runwal Forests premises in restricting entry of an external feeder engaged by me for feeding community animals residing within the premises.
1.	I am a lawful resident and member of the society and have been undertaking feeding, vaccination, sterlisation and medical help of 20 community dogs within the complex out of compassion and responsibility.
2.	Due to my professional commitments, I have appointed my staff as feeder to carry out this activity. However, the management has proposed to restrict entry of such staff, which is arbitrary and discriminatory.
3. The society continues to allow entry of other external personnel such as maids, drivers, housekeeping staff, and delivery agents. Selective restriction only in this case is unjustified and violative of my rights as a member.
4. As per the ABC rule, it is the responsibility of the Resident Welfare Association / management to ensure that community animals are provided with food and water. In the present case, this responsibility lies with the the managing bodies of Runwal Forests, namely Runwal Forests Tower 1, 2, 3, 4 CHSL; Runwal Forests Tower 5, 6, 7 CHSL; Runwal Forests Tower 8 CHSL; Runwal Forest Tower 9 CHSL; along with the builder/overall management but these managing commitees and Runwal facility management team do not take any such responsilibity.
5. I have been voluntarily fulfilling this responsibility. If the society refuses entry to my appointed feeder, then all the 20 dogs residing within the premises would stay hungry as I am the only care taker within the complex.
6.	Denial of food to community animals amounts to cruelty under Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 and is a punishable offence. Further, obstruction or harassment of individuals engaged in feeding community animals is also actionable under law and relevant municipal guidelines.
7.	The present action of the society is not only in violation of statutory provisions but also creates risk of animal distress, which may lead to avoidable incidents within the premises.

Relief Sought:
I respectfully request your office to:
1.	Direct the society management to permit entry of the feeder appointed by me for carrying out feeding of community animals; OR
2.	In the alternative, direct the society to take full responsibility for feeding, medical care, and welfare of all community animals within the premises in compliance with applicable laws
3.	Issue necessary directions to prevent harassment or obstruction in lawful feeding activities

I request your urgent intervention in this matter to ensure compliance with law and to prevent cruelty to animals. Please find attached email from the team that has given ultimatum till 21-04-2026 further which there will be no feeding in the complex.
place_of_incident: Runwal forests, LBS Marg, kanjurmarg west Mumbai

complaint:
Dear Sir/ Madam,
I am writing to urgently seek your intervention in a case of extreme animal cruelty that has occurred in my residential colony.
A street dog and her puppies were being responsibly cared for by a tenant couple in our area. The dogs were properly looked after, vaccinated including anti-rabies, and were known to be non-aggressive and playful.
Due to repeated complaints from certain neighbours, the couple arranged for the mother dog adoption. Within just a week of her relocation, a shocking and brutal incident took place.
In the middle of the night, one of the neighbours, reportedly under the influence of alcohol, entered the premises and violently attacked the mother dog using a heavy wooden bat. He also attempted to attack one of the puppies, who was fortunately saved by the tenants.
The mother dog sustained severe injuries and was immediately taken for medical treatment. As per the veterinary doctor report:
1.She has suffered heavy bleeding
2.She has lost her vision and is now blind
3.She is experiencing repeated seizure- fit attacks
4.Her survival is currently uncertain

The affected tenants have already filed a formal police complaint -FIR, and we also have clear video evidence of the incident.
I am writing this email on their behalf to ensure that this case receives the serious attention it deserves and to help strengthen the ongoing legal action.
This is not just about one dog.. this individual poses a serious threat to animals and potentially to people as well. Such acts of violence must be addressed with strict legal action to prevent recurrence.
I humbly request your support in:
Guiding us on strengthening the legal case under relevant laws
Ensuring strict enforcement of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and applicable IPC sections
Providing any legal assistance or representation if possible
Escalating this matter with local authorities for urgent action
We are ready to share all supporting documents, including:
1. Video evidence of the attack
2. FIR details and witness statements
Your intervention can make a crucial difference in ensuring justice for this innocent animal and in setting a strong example against such cruelty.
We sincerely request you to treat this matter as urgent.
Thank you for your time and support.

Yours Sincerely,
Sonali Tumbare

complaint: I wish to report an imminent case of planned illegal relocation and abandonment of community dogs by certain members of a residential society in Punawale, Pimpri-Chinchwad, Pune. The dogs concerned are peaceful, already sterilised under the Animal Birth Control programme, clearly identifiable by their ear-notching, and there is no documented history or proof of aggression, biting, or veterinary certification against them. Normal playful or territorial behaviour is being wrongly portrayed as aggression to justify forcible removal. Any attempt by private individuals or a society to capture these dogs using vans and dump them elsewhere would constitute illegal abandonment and cruelty, in clear violation of the ABC Rules, 2023 and the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, as sterilised community dogs have the right to remain in their original territory and can only be handled by the municipal authority under legally defined circumstances. I request urgent preventive intervention to restrain such unlawful action and ensure compliance with animal welfare laws.

complaint:
To,
The Respected Authority,
Animal Welfare Board of India

Subject: Complaint regarding cruelty towards stray dog

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, Vansh G. Harke from Sawangi Meghe, Wardha, wish to report an incident of cruelty towards a stray dog. Recently, while I and my sister were outside our colony after dinner, we saw a woman throw a large stone at a stray dog, causing it severe pain.

When we requested her not to harm the animal, she replied that the dogs defecate near her house and further threatened us by saying she would complain against us in the police station.

Stray dogs too have the right to live without cruelty, and such acts are against animal welfare laws. I kindly request the Board to take appropriate action against this inhumane act and spread awareness to prevent such incidents.

Thanking you,

Yours faithfully,
Vansh G. Harke
Sawangi Meghe, Wardha

On 3rd September at around 11 am, at Ganga Apartment, Revati Nagar, a resident of my building 4th floor, directly above my flat chased the dogs to scare them away and later threw bricks and a wooden log from his balcony/terrace directly at them. These objects fell with force and could have caused severe injury or death. Fortunately, the dogs escaped, though they were left terrified.

Direct local municipal authorities and animal welfare officers to monitor this area and protect the community dogs.
Instruct the police to take preventive measures and issue a strict warning to the accused.

complaint:
To the Concerned Authority,
I am writing to formally bring to your attention a deeply concerning incident of animal cruelty that took place within our society, Grandeza. It has been reported that Mr. Abhishek, a resident of Flat No. 209, placed a sofa in a vacant flat and left the door open. Despite the watchman requesting him to close the door, he chose to keep it open. A stray puppy entered the flat and caused minor damage to the sofa. In response to this, Mr. Abhishek allegedly hit the puppy very hard, specifically on its rear legs. As a result, the puppy is now struggling to walk or sit and appears to be in significant pain. We are currently in the process of taking the puppy to a veterinary hospital for urgent medical care. This inhumane act has caused great distress to many of us in the community. We request your immediate attention to this matter, as we do not want such cruelty to be repeated in our society. It is essential that appropriate action is taken to address this behavior and to prevent further harm to any animals within our premises. We kindly seek your guidance on how to proceed further and request that necessary steps be taken against the individual responsible for this act.

complaint:
Dear sir / madam,

I am writing this letter of complaint to bring to your attention a case of animal cruelty near the location of my farmhouse. I live in Aarohi Island https://maps.app.goo.gl/7dGGgHs5DtNcx9DA6 which is a gated community of farmhouse plots. The owner of the farmhouse beside me has forcefully captured a stray dog and leashed it inside their plot. There is no one living in this plot and has only kept this dog in their plot for guard duty. The dog is left for several days without food, water or any kind of socialization. Day and night, the poor dog keeps howling, asking for help. Once a week, an appointed caretaker visits the dog to give him food and water. This is a cruel act of selfishness, and will lead to the death of the dog if action is not taken any time soon. I request you to please look into this matter, and free the dog immediately to save it from eventually dying.

I am willing to break the lock of the gate myself and free the dog, if no action is taken soon. I look forward to your assistance with this case.

Yours sincerely,
Ameya Darshan

complaint: I, Prajwal Kamlesh Dube residing at house number 465/2603, Sant Tukaram Nagar Pimpri 411018. I am compelled to bring to attention the unjust closure of my complaint by police dated 20th December 2024. My initial grievance to Pimpri Chinchwad Police Commissioner highlighted the brutal assault of a stray dog, the intimidation and unlawful assembly outside my residence, by the tenant of House No. 464/2598. My complaint was closed by the Senior Police Inspector Gorakh Kumbhar on false statement given by the accused. Even though I have a CCTV footages to support my grievance. The closure of the case based solely on the statement of a non-complainant is not only disappointing but also raises serious concerns about the impartiality of the investigation.On December 20, 2024, at approximately 7:51 PM, I witnessed a disturbing and unwarranted act of animal cruelty in my neighborhood. Two tenants residing at House No. 464/2598 viciously attacked a stray dog using heavy tiles, hitting the animal multiple times on its back. The attack left the dog severely injured, bleeding from the leg, later unable to walk. Disturbed by this incident, I immediately called the police at 7:53 PM to report the matter and provided details, including the nature of the injuries and the location.In response to my call, two police officers promptly arrived at the scene. However, one of the culprits had fled before their arrival, and the other was taken into custody and subsequently brought to YCM Hospital. At that time, I chose not to escalate the matter further, assuming that the law would handle the situation appropriately. Later that evening, at approximately 11:00 PM, my mother observed a group of individuals, including the culprits roomates and three unknown persons gathering suspiciously near my house.When my mother opened the door to check the situation, they moved towards the room of the accused.I stepped out to assess the situation and noticed five individuals standing nearby. As I passed them, one of them approached me and verbally threatened me, saying that dont file the complaint againt.If you do, we have already spoken to the police, and they have told us to just say that we hit the dog in self-defense. When I ignored his threats and continued walking, he further intimidated me claiming that the police are with them. Such blatant attempts to coerce and intimidate me and my family were alarming. Despite their intimidation, I refrained from any confrontation and trusted that my complaint to the police would ensure justice. Later I learned that the tenants along with Mr Rajan Guhagar, Kartik Wagh and others have decided to kill the dog. I also have a call recording of Mr Rajan Guhagar who threatened me that he would have killed the dog if he was young enough to run behind the dog. Mr Rajan Guhagar had also threatened me in front of police on 20/12/2024 that he would kill the dog if dog is not taken away by municipal corporation. I made three different complaints based on the evidence I had. Unfortunately, I later learned that the police had recorded a statement from a non complainant, Mr Rajan Guhagar and Ajit Rajnikant Makasare, who claimed that the dog attacked him, forcing him to defend himself by throwing a stone. His statement further alleged that my family threatened him to vacate the premises. These claims are not only fabricated but also appear to be made under the influence of the accused individuals to mislead the investigation and protect the perpetrators. The non complainants statement also falsely accuses my family of repeatedly filing frivolous complaints. Such baseless allegations are deeply distressing and divert attention from the original offense of animal cruelty. It is also evident that the police failed to consider my eyewitness account and CCTV footages, the severity of the dogs injuries, and the intimidation faced by my family, raising concerns about bias in the investigation. The accused has got the support of Senior Police Inspector of Sant Tukaram Nagar Police Station, Assistant Sub Inspector Deepak Mahajan who helped the accused to record such statement so that my complaint can be closed. They also falsely narrated my incident to Deputy Commissioner Swapna Gore who in support gave letter to close my complaint.
place_of_incident: 465/2603, Sant Tukaram Nagar Pimpri Pune

complaint:
Im Gavin Dunbar a community dog feeder in Sector 6 Evershine city vasai Maharashtra. Im a partially handicapped person with health issues and I had to stop working few years ago. I take care of my aged parents around 80 years who also have health issues. Daily I feed the society dogs. The society members are against the dogs and want to illegally relocate the dogs and due to this they have a hatred against us who feed the dogs. Society whatsapp groups are filled with all sorts of illegal hate, inflammatory messages and fake news targeting feeders and dogs. Our sector  is a huge gated complex in which there are around 27 buildings divided in 3 societies ie Daisy CHS, Tulip CHS and Lotus CHS. There are around 15 dogs residing inside the sector since birth and all these dogs have been sterilized. This sector has a history of feeder harassment and cruelty to dogs.  We feeders have faced regular harassment including mob threats and attempts of physical assault.
On 30Sep24 we 4 feeders ie me, Loretta Dlomen, Nandkishore Dalvi and his daughter Nikit were discussing about how to send a particular dog to the medical center for observation .
. The members of Daisy society Sector 6 evershine city which is one of the 3 societies sharing the same compound had already gathered in a mob of around 60 people. The mob led by the secetary of Daisy society Mr Vijay Kanojya  threatened to hit and relocate all the dogs that were residing since birth. When the lady feeder Loretta Dlomen objected the secretary charged at her and came very close to her body almost at her face. The other society members pulled him away.  When I tried to record some men  tried to snatch my phone and abused me. Loretta Dlomen  called 112 and also called an animal rights activist Mr Prem Goradia and a social worker Mr Yash Mane . 
When Mr Prem Goradia and Mr Yash Mane arrived to see the situation the mob began abusing them. These individuals were merely trying to resolve the situation peacefully when they were unjustly assaulted. The societys mahila mandal ie lady group came upfront and started abusing all of us especially Loretta.  Now during this entire incident  I did not speak anything to any lady and I was not even in close distance of any lady and This fact can be verified by the society cctv cameras.The secetarys wife Mrs Aarti Kanojia  who was in the forefront abused Ms Loretta Dlomen using a derogatory word randi.  The secetary  Mr Vijay Kanojia then tried to physically attack Loretta. We have video evidence of the secetarys wife abusing Loretta and the secetary attempting to hit lady feeder Loretta video copy attached.
despite calling 112 a number of times the police didnt arrive. As there was a danger to our safety we decided to leave for the police station immediately. The police on our call then came but the mob instead gave their version of the story to the police. When we arrived at Achole police station we were made to wait. The society mob then arrived after 10 minutes and went inside and gave their complaint while we were made to wait despite arriving first to the police station. After waiting for 3 hours we were sent away without getting to register our FIR while the society ladys group registered an FIR against 6 of us. This FIR is done to cover up for their mob attack on us. Please check the CCTV footage of the entire incident of 30Sep24.
Moreover during the entire incident I didnt even speak to any lady nor was I in close distance of any lady and this can be verified by cctv footage.
I was also denied a chance to give an FIR for mob attack on me and only 3 days later the lady  feeder Loretta  got to lodge an FIR after doing multiple rounds of the police station for hours. The police said only 1 FIR can be given from our side so I still am unable to give an FIR although there is a mob attack on me.
The society has also gone to local BJP office and have asked them to speak to us to stop feeding and illegally relocate the dogs. They are harassing us feeders with political pressure.
The society has a Lawyer Dilip Bhandari who tells them that nobody can do anything if we relocate the dogs out and we should relocate the dogs out and not worry about AWBI,  PFA and PETA notice. Being a lawyer he spreads hate and incites people nagainst feeders and community dogs. when we visited Achole police station they too are twisting the law. They are saying we filed a complaint on one stray dog feeder and made that feeder pay for the dog bite according to the new court rule.  in Vasai-Virar officials favour animal haters there are many problems the feeders  face.

complaint:
I reside in VTP Leonara, a newly constructed community where people have moved since March. Recently, there have been threats to relocate the community dogs.
I have tried to inform them about the legal aspects and suggested ways for peaceful coexistence. However, it seems they are not interested in listening or reading any laws, and the entire society appears to be against me. complaint:
My name is Mayur Vasant Shetty. I live in Mahada Colony, in front of Infant Jesus School, Meera Park Society, A-wing 704. After coming home from work every day, I feed the street dogs. I have been facing harassment from the society for a year. The society did not like the street dogs, so they expelled them by force. I agreed to continue feeding them and remained calm. When street dogs come inside the society premises, they come for water. I have kept two bowls, one outside the society and one inside. As soon as the outside bowl finishes, they come inside for water. This is also a problem. I want to take legal action against the society, and your support is crucial to me.

The society has a notice stating that food should be placed 100 meters away from the dogs. However, inside the society, dogs and puppies are hit by cars.
A 72-year-old grandmother lives in the society who feeds the dogs daily with rice and chicken. Society members harass her and have asked her to stop feeding them. Before this, they pressured her owner to remove her from the society.
During rains, the dogs were not allowed inside the society. The society was asked to find a place near the office outside the society premises but they did not respond. The dogs got drenched in the rain.
The security guards are pressured to drive the dogs away with sticks if they come inside the society.
All street dogs in the society are vaccinated and sterilized.
A female dog gave birth to puppies in the society. When they were small, they were tied up and thrown outside the society premises. Even then, when I applied for CCTV footage, they did not support me.
Dogs are hit by cars inside the society. A puppy died as a result.

complaint:
I am submitting this complaint to report serious and repeated acts of cruelty against stray puppies in my locality and the complete negligence of the guardians of a minor responsible for these acts.

I am taking care of six stray puppies in my area. These puppies are harmless and non aggressive and do not pose any threat to residents. Stray animals are protected under Indian law and citizens are legally permitted to feed and care for them.

Despite this, a neighbors minor child has been repeatedly and deliberately abusing these puppies by kicking them forcefully. This behavior is intentional and violent and not accidental or playful. The child has consistently targeted the puppies in sensitive areas causing visible pain fear and distress.

On 03-01-2026 in the morning the child kicked one of the puppies forcefully in the abdominal area. The impact was so severe that the puppy was thrown backward and shortly after started vomiting continuously. This is a clear indication of internal injury or severe trauma and could have resulted in death.

I immediately approached the parents of the child and requested them to stop this cruelty. However they refused to intervene and showed complete disregard for the seriousness of the act. Their response reflected negligence irresponsibility and a lack of basic compassion.

These acts clearly fall under cruelty to animals as defined under Section 11 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act 1960 which prohibits beating kicking or causing unnecessary pain or suffering to any animal. Guardians are responsible for controlling the actions of minors under their supervision and failure to do so amounts to negligence.

The continued violence against these puppies places their lives at constant risk and has caused me extreme distress as a responsible citizen who is trying to protect vulnerable animals. Such behavior if ignored encourages cruelty and poses a serious concern for public safety and moral responsibility.

I strongly request the Animal Welfare Board of India to take immediate action in this matter direct the appropriate authorities to intervene and ensure the safety and protection of the stray puppies so that no further harm is caused.

I declare that the information stated above is true to the best of my knowledge and belief and is submitted in good faith.
